title: Verilator generates errors for unreachable generate/if/endgenerate code
body:
Hi, in contiuation with #2625 , #2754 and #2823, I created this MCVE showing the type of problems I'm facing:

```
module a;
localparam A=1;
generate
if (A==0)
begin
b b_inst(.x(1'b0));
end
endgenerate
endmodule

module b;
endmodule
```
It fails with
```
%Error: a.v:6:11: Pin not found: 'x'
    6 | b b_inst(.x(1'b0));
      |           ^
%Error: Exiting due to 1 error(s)
```
Even though the condition in the if/generate block is false.

I'm running it against my branch which is effectively https://github.com/verilator/verilator/commit/2e158d88c106825d43eb1717799e02385bd5caea plus my [synthesis_off](https://github.com/udif/verilator/tree/synthesis_off) branch (adds support for translate_on and translate_off comments).
The code above runs fine on one of the Big3 simulators, which we currently use at work.

Both the error above and my private branch exists due to the fact that I'm trying to get a large legacy codebase to run under Verilator . I have little control over what I can change in this codebase, esp. when most of the errors are in 3rd party IP.

(You wrote in #24 that you are not interested in a translate_on/translate_off PR. Unfortunately that requires me to maintain that branch and merge the master branch to it once in a while, if I ever want to get our legacy code to compile).
